title: Poecilolepis maritima
intro: Poecilolepis maritima, commonly known as sea olive or sea ilex, is a coastal shrub or small tree that belongs to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Poecilolepis maritima include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Poecilolepis maritima is classified within the family Celastraceae. It belongs to the order Celastrales. The species is a part of the genus Poecilolepis. Poecilolepis maritima is native to Western Australia. The plant is an endemic species found primarily in the southwestern region of the country.
